status: DRAFT — NOT PUBLISHED — SME REVIEW REQUIRED
note: DRAFT end-of-course assessment authored for RA-7001. Every question is grounded in this course's newly authored lesson bodies (data/wordpress-export/courses.json) and paraphrases/applies ANSI/IICRC S520:2024 with clause citations — it does not reproduce the Standard's copyrighted text. Australian English, metric units and AS/NZS context. sourceRef is a review aid only and is not consumed by the seed script. DO NOT seed or publish until a qualified subject-matter expert has reviewed both the course content and these questions for technical accuracy. Structured identically to data/seed/floor-care-quizzes.json. This course carries no IICRC CECs (pending IICRC approval).

quizzes.lessonTitle: End-of-course assessment: Introduction to Mould Identification and Remediation
quizzes.title: Introduction to Mould Identification and Remediation Quiz

questions.questionText: Under ANSI/IICRC S520:2024, what is the goal of mould remediation?
options: To achieve a completely spore-free indoor environment
To return the affected environment to Condition 1 (normal fungal ecology)
To kill all mould with antimicrobial chemicals
To seal the mould behind new plasterboard

questions.questionText: Mould needs a food source, a suitable temperature, time and one further factor. In an occupied Australian home, which factor is normally the one you can actually control?
options: Temperature
The organic food source
Moisture
Time

questions.questionText: Which S520:2024 Condition describes actual mould growth that may be active, dormant, dead, visible or hidden?
options: Condition 1
Condition 2
Condition 3
Condition 4

questions.questionText: A room has settled spores and residual mould material that has drifted from a growth source in the same building, but no actual growth of its own. How is that room classified?
options: Condition 1
Condition 2
Condition 3
It cannot be classified without sampling

questions.questionText: What is the correct role of air and surface sampling during a mould assessment?
options: It replaces the need to find the moisture source
It is a pass/fail test that proves a building is safe
It is one supporting input to the assessment and does not replace locating and fixing moisture
It is mandatory before any visible mould can be called mould

questions.questionText: Which Australian/New Zealand Standard is referenced for the collection of air samples in an indoor air quality assessment?
options: AS/NZS 3760
AS/NZS 4849.1
AS/NZS 1716

questions.questionText: Mains-powered inspection equipment used on an Australian mould job operates on which supply, and what protection is required on a potentially damp site?
options: US 115 V / 15 A, no special protection
230 V / 50 Hz through a 10 A outlet, protected by an RCD/safety switch
240 V, protected only by the building's main fuse
12 V battery only, RCDs are not used in Australia
questions.sourceRef: Course module 5; AS/NZS 3760, Australian 230 V supply

questions.questionText: An occupant tells you they believe the mould is making them unwell. What is the appropriate response for a technician identifying mould?
options: Diagnose the likely illness from the mould species present
Reassure them that mould is harmless
Record the concern and refer them to a medical practitioner; control the exposure, do not diagnose
Refuse to proceed until they provide a medical certificate

questions.questionText: During the S520:2024 §7 inspection process, which activity comes first in building the picture of a mould problem?
options: Removing plasterboard to expose the wall cavity
Gathering the building history and occupant information, then inspecting systematically
Taking air samples in every room
Applying a fungicidal coating to visible growth
